internal_anchors.details: node test.js passes 3381/3381 (covers Section 3 Internal Links). All 71 WIN anchors (including sub-letters 057b/058b) resolve. 12 tab-content divs, 12 tab-bar buttons; every showTab('...') call targets a real tab ID. Not re-walked manually per the 'don't duplicate the test suite' rule.

tab_structure.details: 12 tab-content divs, all unique IDs. overview has class='tab-content active' — correct default. Heading inventory per tab: overview/evaluate/killshots/timestamp/falsify/refs/ai structural; wins/predictions carry the bulk of h2s (8/94). No skipped heading levels observed in spot-checks.

broken.note: Carried forward from 2026-04-19 report. Correct path is /predictions/data/daily_review.json (verified 200). Same file is correctly linked elsewhere. Decider issue is open.

external_links.details: Spot-checked 15 non-citation data-source URLs via curl (-L, 15s timeout): all 200 (intermagnet.org, ngdc.noaa.gov, gml.noaa.gov, cosmos.esa.int gaia, gracefo.jpl.nasa.gov, podaac.jpl.nasa.gov, esa.int Swarm, ncei.noaa.gov WMM, heartmath.org, spacecenter.dk CHAOS-7, earth.esa.int eogateway/swarm, gfz.de champ, imag-data.bgs.ac.uk, ivscc.gsfc.nasa.gov, geodesy.noaa.gov). DOI and publisher domains skipped per spec. The one known-broken daily_review link persists and is already tracked as ISS-1074.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json length 71 matches summary-row count 71. Verdict tally 9+14+22+18+4+4=71 matches wins.json exactly. HTML renders '8 acknowledged failures' and uncounted-failures.json has 8 entries (FAIL-001..FAIL-008). Prose shows '69 claimed wins (+32)' — 69 is dome's V51.1 claim count, 32 is new_in_v51 count; both match computed values. The '69 Claimed Wins' headline is the dome-author-claimed count (intentional; our review adds sub-entries 057b/058b for the dome's duplicate-label disambiguation).

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 WINs have corresponding id='winNNN' (or id='winNNNb') anchors; 0 missing. Verdict text appears in every WIN panel (case-insensitive match). 0 WINs missing tldr_evidence or tldr_verdict. 8 detail_groups are internally consistent (WIN-004/005, 007/022, 008/009, 010/025/068, 011/012, 027/057/069, 040-043+059-061+063 incl. 057b, 045/046/049/050/051). No summary-row asterisk marker for new_in_v51 is emitted by generate-html.js:617 — this was never implemented; the count is surfaced in prose ('+32') and the V51 change-breakdown section rather than per-row. Not a regression; noted as structural.

win_number_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. Dome exposes WIN-001..WIN-069. Dome acknowledges 057 and 058 each carry two claims under one visible label (registry_integrity_notes, 2026-04-17). Our review tracks both claims under each collided number via sub-letter suffixes (057=Two-Zone Disc Topology [Misleading]; 057b=SAA Simultaneous Acceleration / Global cavity re-pressurization [Std Model Explains]; 058=θ=−lonE Unified Angular [Misleading]; 058b=Bedrock Induction Ratio κ=1.677 [Not Demonstrated]). No silent drift of claim titles. PROS-001..PROS-008 on dome page cross-reference WIN-004/007/036/024/035/036/037/038 — these are the 'inflate 67 confirmed' alias pairs documented in prior analysis.

progressive_disclosure.details: All 71 WINs have tldr_evidence + tldr_verdict fields. All 16 is_genuinely_prospective predictions have tldr fields. Three predictions marked prospective:true but NOT is_genuinely_prospective:true (PRED-R002, PRED-094, PRED-TESLA-002) legitimately have no tldr — they are restate/recycled entries, not prospective test items.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: Unchanged from 2026-04-19 report. Both violate dark theme but are long-standing, cosmetic on niche content blocks. Fix: replace #f5f5f5 with var(--card-bg); for the patent thumbnail, either wrap in a light inner container for legibility or set the image background to transparent + var(--card-bg) outer.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt (exists, contains review URL, mentions 'Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model' and dome author URL; count refs are '0 claims survive' and '69 confirmed' — 69 is the dome claim count, consistent with HTML framing). docs/sitemap.xml well-formed <urlset> with main page + llms.txt <loc> entries. docs/robots.txt has 'Allow: /' and 'Sitemap:' directive pointing to the canonical URL. docs/index.html <head> has meta name='description', meta property='og:title', meta property='og:description', and application/ld+json with ClaimReview.

uncounted_failures_schema.details: FAIL-001..FAIL-008, no duplicate IDs, every entry has id+dome_ref+dome_label+what_actually_happened. HTML renders '8 acknowledged failures' — matches file length.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id (192) > max_id (191) — no collision imminent. Single gap EXP-052 is the known historical renumbering (EXP-052 was renamed to EXP-054 on 2026-04-09 to resolve a decider/analyst collision; commit 5e26fc1). Carried forward from every prior report. 0 orphan output files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ (tracker covers all 273 EXP-NNN.json files in git). 0 phantom tracker entries (every status=complete item with output_file exists on disk). 0 pending items older than 48h.

priority_queue_coupling.details: 0 section-new or category-proposal-writeup items in the expansion tracker with status!=integrated. By vacuous quantification all coupled items have queue entries.

workspace_sync_skips.details: No direction-violation skips in 24h — no writer is on the wrong side of the git/workspace boundary. 7 mtime-guard skip(s) are informational: monitor/analyst/category-proposals/CAT-005.json (3x), monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (1x), monitor/decisions/daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json (3x). All three are normal multi-writer paths where the decider committed from its clone, making the workspace copy older.

digest_parse_errors.details: Regenerated digest via 'node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace .' (exit 0) before reading, per the 2026-04-15 mandatory-refresh rule. pending-digest.json errors[] is empty.

digest_coverage.details: 4 latest-cycle review files not yet in processed-reviews.json — within normal pipeline lag (decider has not popped them yet). No prefix concentration (the 2026-04-12 WIN-only-filter bug has not regressed).

build_reproducibility.details: In a fresh /tmp/dome-integrity-clone, 'node build.js html' produces docs/index.html and 'git status' is clean — the committed HTML matches the source data exactly. (The workspace-mount copy of docs/index.html differs from the git version by 88 lines — this is expected FUSE staleness; it means workspace-sync and the decider's last build have not fully propagated to FUSE yet. The authoritative git copy is correct.)

workspace_only_files.details: Two files exist on the workspace (FUSE mount) but are absent from git. SEC-5.0.c1.json (14,604 bytes, mtime 2026-04-20T08:23) is a curmudgeon cycle-1 review for section SEC-5.0; EXP-191.json (14,604 bytes, mtime 2026-04-20T08:23) is an analyst expansion output. Both are at risk of silent data loss if the workspace is recycled. The next workspace-sync run should pick these up, but the decider should be aware — if they are not committed within a few hours, surface on the morning briefing.

claude_md_mechanical.details: Spot-checked 30 'monitor/...' paths referenced in CLAUDE.md — all exist on disk. Schedule table header present. No hardcoded counts detected in CLAUDE.md (all numeric claims are wrapped in query commands per the 'never hardcode' rule).

issues_found.description: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-5.0.c1.json exists on FUSE workspace but not in git — at risk of silent data loss if workspace is recycled.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Copy into a git clone and commit: cp /sessions/stoic-friendly-bell/mnt/dome-model-review/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-5.0.c1.json <clone>/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ && git add && git commit && push. Workspace-sync on its next run should handle this automatically if the file's reason classification is not 'git-owned; direction violation'.

issues_found.description: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-191.json exists on FUSE workspace but not in git — at risk of silent data loss.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Same remediation as the SEC-5.0.c1.json entry above. EXP-NNN.json files are append-only; copying into the clone and committing is safe.

issues_found.description: Broken external link at docs/index.html:2558 — https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/daily_review.json returns 404. Correct path is /predictions/data/daily_review.json.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Already tracked as ISS-1074; carried forward from 2026-04-19. Decider should flip the path to /predictions/data/daily_review.json when it next touches the issue.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded light-theme color #f5f5f5 in sections.json part1.html (WGS84 formula block). Breaks dark theme.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'background: #f5f5f5' with 'background: var(--card-bg)' and 'border-left: 3px solid #666' with 'border-left: 3px solid var(--border)'.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded #fff background in sections.json part6.html (Tesla patent thumbnail card).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either wrap the image in a light inner container so the patent diagram remains legible and set the outer card background to var(--card-bg), or replace 'background:#fff' with a theme-aware equivalent.

summary: Overall warn. Structural integrity is clean — all 3381 test.js assertions pass; 12 tabs wired correctly; all 71 WIN panels and anchors match wins.json; verdict tally (9/14/22/18/4/4) sums exactly to 71; the 2026-04-17 dome registry_integrity_notes disambiguation for WIN-057/058 is mirrored by our 057b/058b sub-entries. Build is fully reproducible from git. Two workspace-only files (SEC-5.0.c1 curmudgeon review, EXP-191 analyst expansion) have not reached git yet and are flagged major — workspace-sync should capture them on its next run. Two long-standing hardcoded-color entries (#f5f5f5, #fff) and one broken external link (already tracked as ISS-1074) are carried forward unchanged. No direction-violation skips, no digest parse errors, no next_id inversion; 4 unprocessed reviews and 7 mtime-guard skips are within normal lag.
